summary: This PR made a one-line GitHub Actions workflow correction in `.github/workflows/build.yml`, changing the `build-ninja` job condition from checking `github.actor == 'doldecomp'` to checking `github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'`. The title indicates this was a revert of a prior `build-ninja` condition change. No PR body, comments, or review discussion were present in the provided slice.

what_changed.change: Updated the `build-ninja` GitHub Actions job guard from `if: github.actor == 'doldecomp'` to `if: github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'`.
Kept the Ninja job scoped to the main doldecomp/melee repository rather than to a specific triggering actor.

smart_moves: Used `github.repository == 'doldecomp/melee'` as the CI condition, which is a repository-level guard and is less dependent on who triggered the workflow.
Applied the fix as a minimal one-line workflow change, reducing risk for unrelated build behavior.
decomp_lessons: For repository-specific CI jobs, prefer guarding on `github.repository` when the intent is to run only in the canonical upstream repo.
Actor-based workflow conditions can be fragile because the triggering actor may vary across pushes, scheduled jobs, bots, maintainers, or automation.
Small CI condition changes can materially affect whether decomp build verification runs, so workflow guards should encode the intended scope directly.
naming_conventions: The workflow job is named `build-ninja` with display name `Ninja`.
The canonical repository identifier used in the workflow condition is `doldecomp/melee`.
follow_up_queries: Find the earlier PR or commit that introduced `if: github.actor == 'doldecomp'` for `build-ninja`.
Search for other GitHub Actions jobs using actor-based conditions that may need repository-based guards.
Check whether fork CI behavior depends on disabling the Ninja container job outside `doldecomp/melee`.
